scrip.label: Ticker (scrip)
scrip.plain: The stock's short trading code on the exchange.
scrip.todo: Use it to look the company up on amarstock.com or dsebd.org.

cat.label: Category (A/B/Z/N)
cat.plain: The exchange's grade for the company. A = pays regular dividend and holds its AGM on time. B = irregular dividend. Z = no dividend / not operating / rule-breaker (worst). N = newly listed.
cat.todo: Keep your core in A. Treat Z as high-risk gambling only.

sector.label: Sector
sector.plain: The industry group (bank, pharma, textile, etc.).
sector.todo: Don't put all your money in one sector — spread it.

ltp.label: LTP — Last Traded Price
ltp.plain: The most recent price one share changed hands at, in Taka. This is 'today's price' every other number compares against.
ltp.todo: Prices here are end-of-day, not live ticks.

eps.label: EPS — Earnings Per Share
eps.plain: The company's yearly profit divided by its number of shares: how much the business earned per share.
eps.todo: Negative EPS means the business is losing money — be very careful.

score.label: Composite manipulation score
score.plain: Our engine blends how far the price stretched, how many daily price-limit hits it had, how fast it ran, and float games into one number. Higher = more manipulation fingerprints.
score.todo: A high score means a syndicate is likely running it — not for your core money.

band.label: Band (score in words)
band.plain: A plain-word bucket for the score: organic = clean, then elevated / strong / extreme as the manipulation signature grows.
band.todo: Only an 'organic' name belongs in a long-term core.

curStretch_pct.label: Stretch above 200-day average (%)
curStretch_pct.plain: How far today's price sits above (or below) its 200-day average price, in %. A big positive number means the price has run far above normal and is prone to snap back.
curStretch_pct.todo: Don't chase a stock stretched far above its 200-day line.

crashP_pct.label: Dump probability (%)
crashP_pct.plain: Our model's estimate of the chance this stock drops hard soon, based on its pump pattern.
crashP_pct.todo: Higher = closer to the dump. Don't be the last buyer.

phase.label: Pump-cycle phase
phase.plain: Where in the pump-and-dump cycle the stock looks to be: DORMANT (quiet) -> ACCUMULATION (quiet buying) -> MID-MARKUP (rising) -> PEAK-DISTRIBUTION (top, insiders selling) -> DUMPING (crashing).
phase.todo: PEAK-DISTRIBUTION and DUMPING are the highest-risk phases in the cycle; ACCUMULATION and DORMANT are the quieter ones.

action.label: Engine action
action.plain: The engine's one-word suggestion for this stock right now (watch / accumulate / exit, etc.).
action.todo: It's a shortcut — still read the trap and way-out before acting.

slowburn.label: Slow-burn pump
slowburn.plain: Flags a slow, quiet pump that hides under the radar — small daily rises instead of obvious limit-ups.
slowburn.todo: A 'yes' is a stealth pump — treat it like any syndicate name.

known_playground.label: Known playground
known_playground.plain: This ticker has been named or fined by the regulator, or is a repeat pump vehicle.
known_playground.todo: A 'yes' is notorious — sleeve-only gambling money at most, never core.

flow.label: Money-flow (our 'sentiment')
flow.plain: Whether trading volume shows real accumulation (buying pressure) or distribution (selling into strength). DSE has no reliable social-media sentiment, so we read the MONEY instead of chatter.
flow.todo: Distribution means the volume shows net selling into strength; accumulation shows net buying.

bsec.label: BSEC investigation flag
bsec.plain: The regulator (BSEC) is actively investigating this name for manipulation — the strongest negative 'sentiment' signal we have.
bsec.todo: Under investigation carries the risk of a trading halt or penalty.

fund_verdict.label: Dividend sustainability
fund_verdict.plain: Can the company keep paying its dividend? SUSTAINABLE = paid from real profit. WATCH = tight. AT-RISK = paying out more than it earns, so a cut is likely.
fund_verdict.todo: Don't hold an AT-RISK stock just for its dividend.

payout_pct.label: Payout ratio (%)
payout_pct.plain: The share of yearly profit paid out as dividend. Over 100% means it's paying more than it earns (often by borrowing or draining reserves) — unsustainable.
payout_pct.todo: Over 100% is a red flag for a future dividend cut.

yield_pct.label: Dividend yield (%)
yield_pct.plain: The yearly dividend as a % of the price — your cash return if the dividend holds.
yield_pct.todo: Only trust a high yield when the fundamentals say SUSTAINABLE.

eps_trend.label: Earnings trend
eps_trend.plain: The direction of yearly earnings: rising (business improving) or falling (worsening).
eps_trend.todo: Falling earnings often come before a dividend cut and a price drop.

sponsor1y.label: Insider (sponsor) trades, 1yr
sponsor1y.plain: Net buying or selling by the company's own directors/sponsors over the last year. Insiders buying = confidence; heavy selling = caution.
sponsor1y.todo: Insiders dumping their own shares is a warning.

beta.label: Beta (market sensitivity)
beta.plain: How much this stock moves versus the whole market. 1.0 = moves with the market; 1.5 = swings about 1.5x as hard both up and down; below 1 = calmer.
beta.todo: High beta means bigger drops in a downturn — size the position smaller.

mkt_corr.label: Market correlation
mkt_corr.plain: How tightly the stock follows the market index: 0 = independent, 1 = moves in lockstep.
mkt_corr.todo: High correlation gives you little diversification benefit.

solvency.label: Solvency (bankruptcy risk)
solvency.plain: A bankruptcy-risk grade from the Altman-Z model. SAFE = financially sound. GREY = watch. DISTRESS = real bankruptcy risk.
solvency.todo: DISTRESS signals real bankruptcy risk, regardless of how cheap the price looks.

solv_trend.label: Solvency trend
solv_trend.plain: Is that financial health RECOVERING, STABLE, or DETERIORATING over recent years?
solv_trend.todo: A GREY name that's DETERIORATING is heading the wrong way.

altman_z.label: Altman-Z score
altman_z.plain: The number behind the solvency grade. Higher is safer: over 2.6 safe, 1.1-2.6 grey, under 1.1 distress.
altman_z.todo: Banks and insurers don't fit this model, so they show as N/A.

earnings_quality.label: Earnings quality
earnings_quality.plain: Whether the reported profit is backed by real cash. PAPER-PROFIT = they book a profit but their cash flow is negative — an accounting red flag.
earnings_quality.todo: PAPER-PROFIT can mean the earnings aren't real — be skeptical.

cash_conv.label: Cash conversion
cash_conv.plain: How much of the reported profit turns into actual operating cash. Near or above 1 is healthy; negative means profit isn't becoming cash.
cash_conv.todo: Negative cash conversion means poor earnings quality.

roe.label: ROE — Return on Equity (%)
roe.plain: Profit as a % of shareholders' money — how well the company uses its owners' capital.
roe.todo: Compare within the same sector. A suspiciously huge ROE can be an accounting artifact.

roa.label: ROA — Return on Assets (%)
roa.plain: Profit as a % of all the company's assets — how efficiently the whole business generates profit.
roa.todo: Low ROA means the business isn't using its assets well.

dsebd_pe.label: P/E ratio (official)
dsebd_pe.plain: Price-to-Earnings from the official exchange: price divided by yearly earnings per share. Roughly the years of earnings you pay for one share.
dsebd_pe.todo: Very high P/E means expensive expectations; negative means no profit.

dsebd_sponsor.label: Sponsor holding % (official)
dsebd_sponsor.plain: The % of the company owned by its sponsors/directors, from the official exchange. More insider ownership = more skin in the game.
dsebd_sponsor.todo: A falling sponsor holding can signal insiders exiting.

src_check.label: Data verified vs exchange
src_check.plain: Did our data match the official exchange record? OK = verified against dsebd.org.
src_check.todo: Trust the numbers more when this says OK.

rating.label: Overall classification
rating.plain: A descriptive classification of the stock — strong-clean, fair, mixed, or high-risk — from combining manipulation, fundamentals and valuation. Not a recommendation to buy or sell.
rating.todo: A summary classification; read the detailed fields for the full picture.

fair_value.label: Fair value (CEILING, not a target)
fair_value.plain: What the stock MIGHT be worth IF it re-rated to peer valuations. DSE keeps quality structurally cheap, so this rarely happens — treat it as an upside ceiling, not a promise.
fair_value.bad: treating it as a guaranteed price
fair_value.todo: Do NOT expect the price to reach this. The real return is dividend + earnings growth.

fv_range.label: Fair-value range
fv_range.plain: The bear-to-bull spread around the fair-value estimate. A wide range means high uncertainty (normal on DSE).
fv_range.todo: A wide range means low confidence.

upside_pct.label: Upside to ceiling (%)
upside_pct.plain: How far the fair-value ceiling sits above today's price. It often looks huge on cheap DSE stocks because it assumes a re-rating that usually never comes.
upside_pct.bad: treating a big number as guaranteed
upside_pct.todo: Discount it heavily — it's a ceiling, not a forecast.

confidence.label: Forecast confidence
confidence.plain: How much to trust the forecast. On DSE this is always LOW — price prediction is unreliable here.
confidence.todo: Low confidence means lean on rules and risk control, not the target.

ENTRY.label: Historical value zone
ENTRY.plain: The price area around the moving-average support where the stock has historically traded in the lower part of its range. Descriptive, not a recommendation.
ENTRY.todo: Price near this area is in its lower historical range.

STOP.label: Historical breakdown level
STOP.plain: A price below which the stock has historically broken down; buffered slightly against fake 'stop-hunt' dips. Descriptive, not a recommendation.
STOP.todo: An observed level where past declines accelerated.

TARGET.label: Target — take profit / trail
TARGET.plain: A reference profit level and trailing plan — where to take some profit or trail your stop up.
TARGET.todo: Take partial profit into strength; let winners run with a trailing stop.

TIMING.label: Seasonal pattern
TIMING.plain: A historical seasonal lean, not a clock: quality names often dip Jun-Aug (post-budget); syndicate names tend to build Nov-Jan and unwind in Jun and Sep.
TIMING.todo: A recurring seasonal pattern, not a rule.

TRAP.label: The trap
TRAP.plain: The specific way this stock could hurt a retail buyer — pump signature, dividend cut, wash volume, penny category, and so on.
TRAP.todo: Read this before buying — it's the 'what could go wrong'.

WAY-OUT.label: What the analysis shows
WAY-OUT.plain: A plain-language summary of the stock's overall profile from the analysis. Descriptive, not a recommendation.
WAY-OUT.todo: A summary of the profile; the decision is yours.

grade.label: Final grade (two-axis)
grade.plain: The two-axis verdict. A-quality = clean price history AND financially sound. ok = acceptable. caution = mixed. high-risk = manipulation history or distress.
grade.todo: Higher grade = a cleaner, financially sounder profile.

comment.label: Summary
comment.plain: A plain-language summary tying all the signals together for this stock.
comment.todo: Read it as the one-paragraph story of the stock.

_sig.label: Signal score (overall quality)
_sig.plain: Our single 'best stock' score: higher = a cleaner, safer, better-positioned stock. It blends grade, rating, manipulation band, solvency, dividend health, phase and money-flow (and penalises dump-risk, paper-profit, known manipulators). The table is sorted by this by default, best first.
_sig.todo: Start reading from the top - but still check each stock's trap and way-out before buying.

syndicate.label: Syndicate
syndicate.plain: A group that coordinates to pump a stock's price up (drawing in retail buyers), then dumps on them. They drive most DSE turnover, so spotting their fingerprints is the whole game.
syndicate.todo: Assume the other side of a hot, stretched stock is a syndicate — not luck.

wash_trade.label: Wash trade
wash_trade.plain: Fake trades where the same players buy and sell to each other to create the illusion of volume and interest. It shows up as abnormal float churn.
wash_trade.todo: Don't trust 'high volume' alone — it can be manufactured.

circuit_breaker.label: Circuit breaker
circuit_breaker.plain: A daily price limit (about plus/minus 10%) that halts a stock from moving further that day. Repeated upper-circuit hits are a pump fingerprint.
circuit_breaker.todo: Many upper-circuit days in a row = a pump, not organic demand.

ma200.label: 200-day moving average
ma200.plain: The average price over the last ~10 months — a slow 'fair trend' line. Price far above it is stretched.
ma200.todo: Use it as the baseline the stretch % measures against.

two_axis.label: The two-axis idea
two_axis.plain: Our core method: judge every stock on TWO questions — (1) Is the price being manipulated? (2) Is it a real, solvent business? You want NO on manipulation and YES on the business.
two_axis.todo: A stock must pass BOTH axes to be core-worthy.

re_rate.label: Re-rating
re_rate.plain: When the market decides to value a stock at a higher multiple (P/E). On DSE, quality stays cheap and rarely re-rates — which is why our 'fair value' is a ceiling, not a target.
re_rate.todo: Don't build a plan on a re-rating that probably won't come.

margin_safety.label: Margin of safety
margin_safety.plain: Buying well below a conservative fair value, so mistakes and bad luck hurt less. Your protection when forecasts are unreliable.
margin_safety.todo: Insist on a discount before buying — it's your cushion.

why_prediction_fails.label: Why price prediction fails on DSE
why_prediction_fails.plain: On DSE, prices are driven by manipulation and thin trading, not fundamentals — so predicting exact prices fails. We focus on risk control, detecting manipulation, and mechanical exits instead.
why_prediction_fails.todo: Trust rules and exits, not price 'targets'.

moving_average.label: Moving average (MA)
moving_average.plain: The average closing price over a set number of recent days, drawn as a smooth line. It filters daily noise to show the underlying trend.
moving_average.todo: Price above a rising MA = uptrend; below a falling MA = downtrend.

ma20.label: 20-day moving average
ma20.plain: Average price over the last ~1 month (20 trading days). A fast line that reacts quickly — marks short-term support and the value zone.
ma20.todo: A dip to the 20-day line in an uptrend is a common re-entry area.

ma50.label: 50-day moving average
ma50.plain: Average price over the last ~2.5 months (50 trading days). A medium-term trend line, often used as a stop reference.
ma50.todo: A close below the 50-day often means the medium trend weakened.

dsex.label: DSEX — DSE Broad Index
dsex.plain: The main Dhaka Stock Exchange index: a weighted average of most listed shares. Shows the overall market direction.
dsex.todo: Use it as the market backdrop — a rising tide helps most stocks.

dses.label: DSES — Shariah Index
dses.plain: The DSE Shariah index — tracks only Shariah-compliant shares.
dses.todo: Relevant if you invest only in Shariah-compliant stocks.

ds30.label: DS30 — Blue-chip Index
ds30.plain: Index of 30 large, established DSE companies — a gauge of the big, liquid 'blue-chip' stocks.
ds30.todo: Compare DS30 vs DSEX to see if big caps or the broad market leads.

ycp.label: YCP — Yesterday's Close
ycp.plain: The previous trading day's closing price. Today's change is measured against it.

support.label: Support
support.plain: A price level where buyers have repeatedly stepped in, tending to stop further falls.
support.todo: The 'historical value zone' is a support area.

resistance.label: Resistance
resistance.plain: A price level where sellers have repeatedly appeared, tending to cap further rises.
resistance.todo: The 'historical upper range' is a resistance area.

breadth.label: Market breadth
breadth.plain: How many stocks rose vs fell on the day. Broad gains (many up) are healthier than an index pulled up by a few.
breadth.todo: Weak breadth on an up day = a fragile rally.

volume.label: Volume
volume.plain: Number of shares traded in a period. High volume shows real interest; a move on high volume is more convincing.
volume.todo: Beware a price rise on abnormally high volume — can be wash trading.

nav.label: NAV — Net Asset Value
nav.plain: A company's assets minus liabilities, per share — the book value behind each share.
nav.todo: Price far above NAV = expensive on assets; far below can be value or trouble.

floor_price.label: Floor price
floor_price.plain: A regulator-set minimum below which a stock can't trade. It traps sellers and freezes real price discovery.
floor_price.todo: A stock stuck on its floor is effectively un-sellable.

free_float.label: Free float
free_float.plain: The share of stock actually available to public trading (excludes locked sponsor/government holdings). Low float is easy to manipulate.
free_float.todo: Low free float + a sharp run-up is a classic pump setup.

turnover.label: Turnover
turnover.plain: Total taka value traded (price × volume). Market turnover shows how active and liquid trading is.

volatility.label: Volatility
volatility.plain: How much a price swings. High volatility = bigger, faster moves both ways = higher risk.
volatility.bad: very high for a core holding
volatility.todo: Size positions smaller in high-volatility names.

liquidity.label: Liquidity
liquidity.plain: How easily you can buy or sell without moving the price. Thin stocks are hard to exit at a fair price.
liquidity.todo: Prefer liquid names; illiquid ones trap you.

unrealized_pl.label: Unrealized profit/loss
unrealized_pl.plain: Your on-paper gain/loss on holdings you still own (current price vs your buy price). It becomes real only when you sell.
unrealized_pl.todo: Green = paper gain, red = paper loss — nothing is locked in until you sell.

npl_proxy.label: Bad-loan ratio (NPL proxy)
npl_proxy.plain: For banks: specific provisions ÷ loans — a floor estimate of classified/uncollectable loans. Banks under-provision and reschedule, so the true bad-loan rate is usually higher.
npl_proxy.todo: High or rising = asset-quality trouble — a bank solvency red flag.

prov_drag.label: Provision drag
prov_drag.plain: For banks: loan-loss provisions as a % of profit-before-provision — how much of the bank's earnings bad loans are eating.
prov_drag.todo: Over 100% means bad loans wiped out the operating profit.

life_fund_bn.label: Life fund (৳ bn)
life_fund_bn.plain: For life insurers: the accumulated policyholders' fund — premiums plus investment income, minus claims, surrenders and expenses, built up over the years. The core measure of a life insurer's strength.
life_fund_bn.todo: A negative (deficit) life fund is severe distress — obligations exceed the fund.

fund_cagr.label: Life-fund growth (%/yr)
fund_cagr.plain: For life insurers: how fast the life fund is growing per year. Growth means inflows (premium + returns) beat outflows (claims + expenses). A shrinking fund means the opposite.
fund_cagr.todo: A steadily falling fund signals the book is running off faster than it is replenished.

life_claims_ratio.label: Claims / premium (%)
life_claims_ratio.plain: For life insurers: net claims and surrenders as a % of premium collected. Above 100% means payouts exceed new premium, so the fund leans on investment income to stay level.
life_claims_ratio.todo: Persistently high claims-to-premium with a falling fund is a warning sign.

life_mgmt_ratio.label: Management expense / premium (%)
life_mgmt_ratio.plain: For life insurers: total management expenses as a % of premium. High expense ratios erode the fund and breach the regulator's expense limits.
life_mgmt_ratio.todo: Very high expense ratios at small/new insurers eat into policyholder value.

navps.label: NAV per share
navps.plain: Net asset value per share — the company's assets minus liabilities, per share. The book value behind each share.
navps.todo: Price below NAV can be value; far above = pricey on assets.

rsi14.label: RSI (14-day)
rsi14.plain: Momentum gauge 0-100. Below 30 = oversold, above 70 = overbought. DSE backtest: oversold (RSI under 30) is our strongest measured bounce signal (+7.8 edge); overbought has NO edge here.
rsi14.todo: Only the oversold side carries measured signal on DSE.

macd.label: MACD state
macd.plain: Trend-momentum indicator (12/26/9). 'Bullish' = MACD line above its signal line. DSE backtest: the crossover itself showed NO edge — read it as trend description only.
macd.todo: Descriptive only; crossovers did not predict on DSE.

bollinger.label: Bollinger position
bollinger.plain: Where price sits vs its 20-day average ±2 standard deviations. Below the lower band = statistically washed-out (DSE backtest: +6.2 edge for a bounce); above upper = stretched (no edge).
bollinger.todo: The lower-band side is the one with measured DSE signal.

vol_ann_pct.label: Volatility (annualized %)
vol_ann_pct.plain: How wildly the price swings in a normal year. 25% = calm; 60%+ = violent swings — position smaller.

max_dd_5y_pct.label: Max drawdown (5y %)
max_dd_5y_pct.plain: The worst peak-to-bottom fall in the last 5 years — the pain a holder actually endured.
max_dd_5y_pct.todo: Assume a repeat is possible; can you sit through it?

var95_daily_pct.label: Daily VaR 95%
var95_daily_pct.plain: On the worst 1-in-20 days, the stock loses at least this much. A one-day risk yardstick.
var95_daily_pct.todo: A quick feel for single-day downside.

days_to_exit_1L.label: Days to exit ৳1 lakh
days_to_exit_1L.plain: Trading days needed to sell a ৳100,000 position at 10% of normal daily volume without moving the price. Liquidity in practical terms.
days_to_exit_1L.todo: Illiquid names trap you exactly when you need out.

ddm_value.label: Dividend-model value (crude)
ddm_value.plain: A rough intrinsic value from the cash dividend (Gordon model, 14% required return, growth from the EPS trend). Only meaningful for steady dividend payers.
ddm_value.todo: A crude anchor, not a target — ignore for non-payers.

ddm_gap_pct.label: Dividend-model gap (%)
ddm_gap_pct.plain: How far the dividend-model value sits from the current price. Positive = price below the dividend-implied value.
ddm_gap_pct.todo: One more valuation lens; weakest of the three (P/NAV, fair-value, DDM).

sector_rs_rank.label: Sector strength rank
sector_rs_rank.plain: Where this stock's sector ranks among all sectors by the last 60 days' average return — sector rotation in one number.
sector_rs_rank.todo: Strong sectors carry their members; weak sectors drag them.

peer_rank.label: Rank among sector peers
peer_rank.plain: This stock's position within its own sector by analysis score (1 = strongest peer).
peer_rank.todo: Prefer the strongest name in a sector over the laggards.

inst_hold.label: Institutional holding (%)
inst_hold.plain: Share held by institutions (funds, banks). The 'smart money' block of the shareholder register.
inst_hold.todo: Watch the CHANGE more than the level.

foreign_hold.label: Foreign holding (%)
foreign_hold.plain: Share held by foreign investors — the most selective money on DSE.
foreign_hold.todo: Foreign exit waves have historically led market slides.

smart_flow.label: Smart-money flow (MoM pp)
smart_flow.plain: Month-over-month change in institutional + foreign holding, in percentage points. Positive = professionals accumulating; negative = professionals leaving.
smart_flow.todo: Institutions exit quietly before trouble — a falling number is an early warning.

most_predictive_factor.label: Most predictive factor (this stock)
most_predictive_factor.plain: Of all measured indicators, the one whose level best correlated with this stock's NEXT 10-day moves across its full history. Market-wide the winner is distance from the 200-day average (mean reversion).
most_predictive_factor.todo: Watch this factor on this stock more than the others.

most_predictive_ic.label: Its correlation (IC)
most_predictive_ic.plain: Spearman rank correlation between that factor's level and the next 10-day return. Negative = high readings led to weaker returns (mean reversion). Beyond ±0.05 is meaningful for daily data.
most_predictive_ic.todo: Small numbers are normal; direction matters more than size.

wyckoff_state.label: Cycle state (now)
wyckoff_state.plain: Where the stock sits in its accumulation → markup → distribution → markdown cycle, detected from DSE history since 1999 (weekly price+volume). ACCUM = quiet base-building; MARKUP = rising after breakout; DIST = topping with heavy churn.
wyckoff_state.todo: Backtest caveat: only ~27% of breakouts reach +25%, but winners are large — a low-probability, high-payoff pattern that worked mainly in bull markets.

state_weeks.label: Weeks in current state
state_weeks.plain: How long the stock has been in its current cycle state. Compare with its own typical accumulation length.
state_weeks.todo: An accumulation far longer than typical often means a dormant base, not an imminent move.

typical_accum_weeks.label: Typical accumulation (weeks)
typical_accum_weeks.plain: Median length of this stock's own historical accumulation phases before breakout.
typical_accum_weeks.todo: Time reference for the current base.

p_markup25.label: P(markup ≥ +25%) (%)
p_markup25.plain: From this stock's own history: share of detected accumulation breakouts that went on to gain at least 25%. A per-stock success probability, not a promise.
p_markup25.todo: Historical frequency only; most breakouts fail — position accordingly.

accum_month_mode.label: Accumulation month (mode)
accum_month_mode.plain: Calendar month in which this stock's accumulation phases most often began. Market-wide, accumulations cluster May-June (post-budget) and Nov-Dec.

dist_month_mode.label: Distribution month (mode)
dist_month_mode.plain: Calendar month in which this stock's distribution (topping) phases most often began. Market-wide, distribution clusters Sep-Oct and Jan — typically 2-4 months before AGM season.
dist_month_mode.todo: Watch for churn in this month if the stock has run up.

agm_month.label: AGM month
agm_month.plain: The month this company usually holds its AGM. Distribution has historically clustered 2-4 months before AGMs market-wide (pump into declaration, sell before/at record date).
agm_month.todo: Combine with the cycle state: run-up + approaching AGM = classic distribution window.

next_record_date.label: Next record date
next_record_date.plain: The upcoming record date (entitlement cut-off) announced to DSE, when available. Prices often behave differently before vs after it.
next_record_date.todo: After the record date, the dividend reason to hold expires — watch for post-record selling.

p_nav.label: Price / NAV
p_nav.plain: Share price ÷ NAV per share. Under 1 = trading below book value; over 1 = above it.
p_nav.todo: For a mutual fund, P/NAV below 1 means it trades at a discount to what it holds.

diversification.label: Diversification
diversification.plain: Spreading money across different stocks and sectors so one bad holding doesn't sink you.
diversification.todo: Don't put most of your money in one stock or one sector.
